{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 70,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "DP impulse lines sloped incorrectly for service — gas taps routed below pipe preventing condensate drainage back to process, liquid taps routed on top trapping gas pockets",
    "Incorrect impulse line routing will cause false DP readings due to accumulated condensate or gas in lines",
    "Installation does not comply with API RP 551 impulse line routing requirements for differential pressure transmitters"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Rework impulse tubing to achieve correct slope: gas service taps above pipe with lines sloping down toward process, liquid service taps below pipe with lines sloping up toward process or drain",
    "Verify slope is continuous with no sags or traps that could collect condensate or gas",
    "Re-inspect impulse line routing after rework before proceeding to closeout"
  ],
  "rationale": "API RP 551 requires impulse lines for gas service to be sloped so condensate drains back to the process (taps above pipe), and liquid service lines to be sloped so gas vents back to process (taps below pipe). The observed condition is reversed for both services, which will cause measurement error and potential transmitter malfunction. This is a classic installation defect that must be corrected before closeout.",
  "references": ["API RP 551 - Process Measurement", "Manufacturer installation manuals for DP transmitter impulse line requirements"]
}
